title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on Oden, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Whitebeard, Unknown person #3, Kinemon as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The world is made up of four regular seas and one long one.

dialogue.speaker: Oden
dialogue.text: · Oden's voyage continues swimmingly!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And apparently it's as round as a marble!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: How wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So this is how small wano is compared to the rest of the world, huh...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Where were you born, Tuki?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't know. My parents always said their home- land was wano.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't really have anywhere else to go back to.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To be honest, I don't think this ship will venture anywhere near Wano for some time.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What a shame.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's okay! When I'm with you, I feel like I've already reached my destination.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hmm? Why's that?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Ufufu® Why, indeed.:)

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Oi, where's Lord old--

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Get back here, you stupid cat!

dialogue.speaker: Whitebeard
dialogue.text: Whitebeard had sworn enemies, too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: It's the Marines! Run!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: That's white- beard's ship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Why don't we just fight them!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They're part of an organization. Even if we fight them, more will come to take their place.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So there's an organization out there that spans the whole world?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They've probably got some crazy guys on their team!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Judge that for yourself as we continue to travel around the world.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: For your country is still free!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 28 years ago, the second year of Oden's voyage:

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He's born!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: TAMAGO!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Kombu!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Chiklwa!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hogya! Hogya!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: TSUKUNE!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oi! Knock it off! He needs a better name!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A name that conveys that he will be invincible! Your name, son, is...

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: Kozuki

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Momo- Nosuke!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Lord Oden, you're on wanted posters now. Abroad, you've become a fine criminal.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: For the sake of lady toki and Lord MOMONOSUKE, shouldn't you return to Wano sometime soon?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 120... I haven't yet found the answer I'm looking for!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: And we'll be fine!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Seriously, though... even though they have these news- paper things, there's never any information about Wano in 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's just no way for information to leave the country. Same as Zou.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I hope kin and the others are alright.

dialogue.speaker: Whitebeard Pirates
dialogue.text: You're appointing Division Captains?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We've got a lot more people in the crew nowadays. How about it? We'll split into five divisions.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: That's a great idea!

dialogue.speaker: Whitebeard Pirates
dialogue.text: I wanna be a Division Captain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Me too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: You're getting the second division!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Er, I don't really want- I mean...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Do it!

dialogue.speaker: Toki
dialogue.text: From time to time, people

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're not really fishermen, are you? You're pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: would come to the ship. Just as we did.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: PLEASE! I don't have anywhere else to go!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: What's your name, boy?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Teach!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Another orphan.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: GOTCHA. Get on board!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: but no matter how far I sail,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Did you see that?! Something was flying out there!

dialogue.speaker: Whitebeard Pirates
dialogue.text: Captain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We've got to scout the area first!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I still feel that I haven't seen enough!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I haven't the slightest clue how to bring this adventure of mine to an end.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 64 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
